lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Why, hello there, [lad/lass]. Me friends call me Ned. I was a man of the sea, but it's past me now. Could I be making or selling you some rope?

sections.title: After completion of Dragon Slayer I

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Hello again, [lad/lass]. Something I can help you with? Want to buy some rope perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you get back from Crandor?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: I got towed back by a passing friendly whale.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Anyway, would you like me to make some rope while you're here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me back to Crandor again?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Aye, that I could. I'll meet you at the Port Sarim docks again. I'll try to steer a little better this time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I would like some rope.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well, I can sell you some rope for 15 coins. Or I can be making you some if you gets me 4 balls of wool. I strands them together I does, makes em strong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You make rope from wool?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Of course you can!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you needed hemp or jute.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Do you want some rope or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, please sell me some rope.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: There you go, finest rope in Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. Hmmm, do you know something? This rope has a funny smell.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, It reminds me of...yaks. I was travelling in the Fremennik Isles recently and, do you know what? They made rope out of yak's hair.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: What a coincidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There was a lady over there who sold wool, she said she traded rope for the wool.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Who would have thought it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think you make rope at all. I think you trade balls of wool for rope.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Oh, I confess. You have found the truth. I used to make the rope myself, but my fingers aren't as nimble as they used to be and so many people wanted my rope. Demand was so high I had to find another

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: source. But I couldn't tell anyone since Ned's Handmade Rope (100% Wool) is a high profile brand now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, well, the wool is still pretty good, though yak hair makes fine rope. I don't suppose any harm will come of it.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Thanks. Here take this, a free sample! Please, don't tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's always good to keep on the good side of a sailor. Your secret is safe with me.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Oh, if you're in the Isles again, tell Jofridr that her Neddie will be there soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will do that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some balls of wool. Could you make me some rope?

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Sure I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a little more than I want to pay.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well, if you ever need rope that's the price. Sorry. An old sailor needs money for a little drop o' rum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will go and get some wool.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Aye, you do that. Remember, it takes 4 balls of wool to make strong rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks Ned, I don't need any.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well, old Neddy is always here if you do. Tell your friends. I can always be using the business.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Well done!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Good work! I have new information to share with you. Listen carefully, now!

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: A place 65 steps forward and 17 to the right needs an inspection. The weather vane up high will guide you in the right direction.

lines.speaker: Ned
lines.text: Under the ground lies the next clue you seek. Getting it out requires a little technique.
